counter_argument: Triggering the honest-client-desync path requires a client engine that has ALREADY diverged (a separate determinism bug) or a deliberately tampered client; with all-honest in-sync clients the reported cursor always equals the deterministic successor, so the gate is correct in the common case. Stakes are an anonymous casual artillery game with no auth/PII/money (ADR 0006), and impact is bounded to the attacker's own room. So it is a latent structural SPOF, not a live exploit.
rationale: PRIMARY of group referee-cursor-trust. The referee runs no physics and has no independent turn-order source of truth — it stores the submitter's reported nextActiveIndex verbatim as the gate for every later turn. The proper fix (authoritative referee that replays the shared engine over the log, per SPEC §5) is an ADR-grade architecture choice, not a mechanical patch; the cheap hardening (reject a reported seat that is dead/out-of-range/non-successor) only narrows it. Routed to decision-required so the thin-vs-authoritative-referee tradeoff is decided deliberately.

counter_argument: A member can already join and grief a room they are in (no-auth posture, ADR 0006); pointing the referee cursor at another seat in your OWN room is within that already-accepted trust boundary and has no cross-room/cross-tenant reach. Honest clients recompute the real successor, so for an all-honest table the rendered game is unaffected. The marginal harm over the accepted baseline is low for a casual game.
rationale: Security framing of the same root as reliability-003: the referee's only authz gate (active seat == acting seat) rests on peer-influenced state. Combined into group referee-cursor-trust; same remediation. ADR-grade response → decision-required.

counter_argument: Effect is referee-accepted out-of-phase buy rows scoped to the attacker's own room; honest engines route/ignore an out-of-phase buy deterministically so the game outcome is unaffected (log noise only). Replay-safe and within the accepted no-auth posture.
rationale: Same root as the group: the ROUND_OVER buy regime is gated only by the client-asserted roundOver flag because the referee derives no phase from the log. Folds into referee-cursor-trust; resolved by the same authoritative-vs-thin referee decision.

counter_argument: Bounded to the caller's OWN room's durable record; sanitizeScoreboard already range/shape-checks; no in-game state or cross-room impact, consistent with ADR 0006. Prior backlog #9 added membership+roster-range checks — this is the residual log-verification gap, not a regression.
rationale: Real, concrete forgeable durable record: finish_game writes match_scores/winner from the request body with no cross-check against the action log. Clears the low bar (conf 0.75 + concrete remediation: derive winner from log or explicitly document as advisory).

counter_argument: Not XSS (sink is CSSOM/canvas which ignore invalid values); impact is payload bloat in the shared room row. Bounded to rooms the member is in.
rationale: Concrete and cheap: color is validated only for non-empty string in all three write paths (name is capped at 20, color is not). Unbounded string persisted to shared JSONB and broadcast to peers. Clears low bar; effort S. Fix in the same shared validator the name uses.

counter_argument: AudioEngine is a page singleton (not a per-game leak); the AudioContext/listeners persisting for the session is by design. Only the stale doc reference + mid-burn napalm node leak at teardown are real.
rationale: Concrete maintenance trap + minor leak: napalmStart() doc references a reset() that does not exist, and sustained napalm source/gain nodes are not torn down if a game ends mid-burn. Clears low bar; effort S.

counter_argument: Fail-open is the correct liveness choice; the gap is only observability of the fail-open path. Overlaps the observability lens but is a distinct resilience concern.
rationale: Concrete: enforceRateLimit fails open on any RPC error with only a console.error, so a chronically-degraded limiter (missing table, perms regression) silently disables ALL per-IP limiting unnoticed. Remediation: distinguishable structured log/metric on the fail-open path (+ optional circuit breaker). Clears low bar.

POSITIVE CONTROL, not a defect: the only Math.random in the client is network backoff jitter, correctly OUTSIDE shared/ and never in the replayed path, so it cannot affect lockstep determinism. Recorded as evidence the invariant holds; nothing to fix. (The preventive CI-guard action it implies is captured by reliability-009.)

counter_argument: Requires a black-holed (hung, not erroring) connection during fire-recovery specifically; an erroring fetch is handled. But the FIRE_TIMEOUT watchdog fires once then defers to an unbounded fetch, so the stuck-lock it exists to prevent can still occur.
rationale: Concrete and real: no fetch in client/src uses an AbortController/timeout (grep-confirmed), so a hung resyncLog in recoverStuckFire never resolves and the player is trapped in 'Sending…' with no recovery but reload. Remediation: bound supabase reads / edge POSTs with an AbortController timeout and treat resync-overrun as failure.

counter_argument: The 10k-tick cap reportedly cannot be hit given the strictly-converging settle/flight models (see reliability-007), so the wedge is a defensive edge case, not an observed live freeze. Likelihood is low; impact (silent permanent per-client freeze for the match) is high.
rationale: Real recovery gap: on hitting the tick cap, tickToCompletion only console.errors and returns with the engine left in FIRING/RESOLVING, after which flushPendingActions never drains again — a silent permanent freeze with no UI signal. Remediation: force a recoverable phase or surface a fatal-desync banner on cap.

counter_argument: observed:false — the agent itself notes settleStep currently cannot fail to converge (strictly-downward sand model), so this is a speculative defensive-parity suggestion, not a demonstrated defect.
rationale: Below the calibrated confidence gate after discounting the inferred/speculative basis. Routed to investigate (defensive RESOLVING-phase iteration cap) rather than filed; folds naturally into reliability-006's recovery work if picked up.

counter_argument: Not a desync (fire still applies in seq order, engine phase-gates correctly); effect is a brief HUD flicker + the per-shot watchdog being defeated for a one-seq window. Genuinely minor.
rationale: Real but below the low-severity bar (needs conf >=0.75 for a low to be issue-eligible; calibrated to 0.65). Routed to investigate. If the firing-lock work in reliability-005/006 is picked up, fix this together (key the lock release on the local submit's own seq).

counter_argument: It is a preventive control, not a current defect — the invariant already holds. But preventing silent regression of the prime invariant is high-value and cheap.
rationale: Actionable preventive: add a CI/lint guard (or scripts/checks harness) that fails the build if Date.now/performance.now/Math.random/new Date appears under shared/src/engine. Protects lockstep determinism from silent regression. Clears low bar; effort S.

counter_argument: Logs are Supabase-dashboard-access-gated; no secret/key is exposed, only Postgres internals (codes, constraint/column names). Low real exposure.
rationale: Concrete batch fix across ~10 functions: log error.message (the pattern already correct in _shared/mod.ts:91) instead of the full Supabase error object. Clears low bar; effort S.

counter_argument: Modulo bias is cosmetic (negligible A–D overweight). Enumeration is throttled by the 20/min join_room limit and rooms are ephemeral with no PII/stakes; only distributed/IP-rotating brute force is practical.
rationale: The actionable core is the small 36^4 code space for private rooms. Remediation: 6-char codes (36^6) or a per-room failed-join lockout (+ optional rejection-sampling to remove bias). Clears low bar; effort S.

counter_argument: Current tree is clean (no real .env committed) and the global .env catch-all already ignores the file; this is belt-and-suspenders only.
rationale: Cheap defense-in-depth around the file that holds the service-role key: add explicit 'supabase/functions/.env' and '.env.local' entries to .gitignore. Clears low bar; effort S.

counter_argument: No PII/auth tokens in the app and no confirmed XSS, so the practical exposure (clickjacking, MIME-sniff) is modest for a casual game.
rationale: Concrete, conventional hardening: add X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy (and ideally a CSP) to nginx.conf and a netlify.toml [[headers]] block. Clears low bar; effort S.

counter_argument: Same major version, backward-compatible API; currently resolves to the identical 2.107.0. Operational drift risk, not a security defect today.
rationale: Below the low-severity issue bar (conf 0.7 < 0.75). Routed to investigate: pin client @supabase/supabase-js to match the esm.sh edge pin, or document the manual sync step.

counter_argument: esm.sh with an exact version tag is low mutation-risk and Supabase caches modules; the gap is only integrity-hash pinning, an industry best-practice rather than a live vuln.
rationale: Concrete supply-chain hardening: commit a deno.lock capturing integrity hashes for the esm.sh import (or vendor supabase-js locally), and update the .gitignore comment. Clears low bar; effort M.

counter_argument: Only rooms that (a) contain a CPU seat AND (b) use the rematch flow are affected; a fresh game is unaffected, and the failure is a recoverable freeze (leave to lobby), not data corruption. So blast radius is the bot-rematch path specifically.
rationale: ANCHOR of group rematch-ai-flag. restart_game/index.ts maps players without spreading the `ai` field, so every bot seat in a rematch room loses its bot designation; NetworkClient.botByTank is then empty and no client drives the seat — the game silently freezes on bot turns. Guaranteed break of a real feature with a one-line fix (`...(p.ai ? { ai: p.ai } : {})`). Highest-value actionable finding of the run.

counter_argument: It is the test for migration-004, not a separate defect; on its own it is a coverage gap, not a live failure.
rationale: Combines into rematch-ai-flag: a restart_game test asserting newPlayers preserves ai:'medium' for a bot seat would have caught migration-004 and prevents regression. Ship the fix + this characterization test together.

counter_argument: Provisional high overstates it for a hobby game: a realistic lobby has a handful of waiting rooms, so N is small and the sequential reaps are a few round-trips, not dozens. The pattern is genuinely O(N)-per-poll though and degrades under load.
rationale: Real N+1 on the polled list_rooms endpoint: a per-room reap loop issues one awaited DELETE/UPDATE per stale room. Calibrated high->medium. Remediation: batch into one DELETE ... WHERE id = ANY(...) + one UPDATE (or a reap_stale_rooms() plpgsql), and pre-filter by created_at. effort M.

counter_argument: Runs once per AI turn (600ms-gated, aiActedKey-deduped), not per frame, so it is a single synchronous burst, and on desktop it is imperceptible. Only low-end mobile sees jank. Reducing the grid changes AI plans (still deterministic) so it must be done carefully vs the harnesses.
rationale: Real main-thread cost: hard-difficulty searchShot sweeps up to ~13.9k simulateImpact probes (171 angles x 81 powers), each up to 1600 sweepCollide steps over the 720k bitmap. Remediation: Web Worker, coarser grid + early-exit (determinism-preserving), or budget the search. effort M.

counter_argument: Bounded to one ~720kB allocation per turn-ending action; modern GC handles short-lived large buffers, so the practical jank is modest in a 2P game. But it is pure avoidable work on the time-critical submit path.
rationale: computeNextSeat clones the entire GameEngine (incl. terrain.slice() = 720k bytes) every turn-ending action just to derive the next active seat. Remediation: derive the next alive seat from a cheap array scan over state.tanks instead of cloning+ticking. Intersects referee-cursor-trust (this clone produces the trusted nextActiveIndex) but the perf fix is independent. effort M.

counter_argument: Bursts are transient and CanvasGradient objects are small; the GC pressure is real but intermittent, not a steady-state cost.
rationale: Group gradient-caching: drawExplosions allocates a fresh RadialGradient per burst per frame. Calibrated medium->low (transient). Cache the fixed-geometry gradient after the grow phase.

counter_argument: Lower magnitude than 003; scorches are longer-lived but fewer simultaneous.
rationale: Group gradient-caching: drawScorches allocates a fresh RadialGradient per scorch per frame; geometry is fixed after spawn, only alpha changes (apply via globalAlpha on a cached gradient).

counter_argument: One small object per frame; cost is minor but the fix is free and the sun is a pure constant.
rationale: Group gradient-caching: drawSun rebuilds a RadialGradient every frame though its parameters are invariant. Cache it on the Renderer alongside skyGradient.

counter_argument: Napalm-only and engine ticks are cheap; minor GC pressure during a time-limited weapon. Determinism-sensitive — the sorted snapshot order must be preserved exactly.
rationale: Concrete: syncFire rebuilds+sorts a fresh array every burning tick even when only `life` values decremented. Remediation: in-place decrement + rebuild only on ignite/expire (fireDirty flag), preserving sort order; re-run scripts/checks. Clears low bar.

counter_argument: Minor over-fetch on a per-turn endpoint; bandwidth saving is small at hobby scale.
rationale: Group edge-fn-perf: submit_action does select('*') but reads only players/active_player_index/turn/status. Narrow the projection.

counter_argument: Deno isolate reuse is not guaranteed; the win depends on warm isolates. Construction overhead is small per call.
rationale: Group edge-fn-perf: getServiceClient builds a new client every call (twice per submit_action). Remediation: module-level singleton (env present in a deployed isolate), converting startup failure to MissingEnvError.

counter_argument: The agent itself states the true cost is sub-microsecond (empty for..of loops). Below the bar for an issue; marginal polish.
rationale: Real but trivial: add an early-return length guard to EffectsRenderer.update mirroring draw(). Recorded; not worth filing this run. Fold in opportunistically if that file is touched.
POSITIVE CONTROL: TerrainRenderer correctly gates the 720k rebuild on terrainVersion (prior backlog P2-8 properly fixed) — no per-frame hash/scan. Recorded as evidence; nothing to fix.

counter_argument: The defaults only fire for legacy/malformed option rows; in the common path real options are present, so the divergent literals rarely matter. But when they do, a desynced default seeds different gravity/wind caps = silent lockstep desync.
rationale: ANCHOR of group determinism-drift-guards. gravity 0.15 / maxWind 10 are hand-copied across 5 sites incl. NetworkClient.ts which already imports GRAVITY and bypassed it. Remediation: client uses GRAVITY/MAX_WIND (no literals); edge side gets _shared DEFAULT_GRAVITY/DEFAULT_MAX_WIND constants with a MUST-match comment.

counter_argument: All 23 fields are handled correctly TODAY; this is a latent trap on the NEXT field addition, not a present defect. The clone has exactly one caller.
rationale: Group determinism-drift-guards: GameEngine.clone() hand-enumerates 23 private fields + every GameState prop; a future field omitted from clone() silently desyncs the networked next-seat derivation with no type error. Remediation: a key-parity/serialize-roundtrip harness assertion so clone completeness can't rot.

counter_argument: Currently agrees with the engine (one accessory exists); the divergence only manifests when a 2nd AccessoryType is added — a latent trap, not a live bug.
rationale: Group determinism-drift-guards: the 'battery' accessory allowlist is hardcoded in 2 referee sites (validate.ts, index.ts) duplicating engine AccessoryType; adding an accessory silently 400s/strips it over the network while hot-seat accepts it. Remediation: one _shared ACCESSORY_TYPES set + MUST-match comment/CI grep.

counter_argument: Harmless dead code; no runtime impact. Pure cognitive overhead / false source-of-truth.
rationale: Concrete dead code: shared/src/index.ts barrel has zero importers (all consumers use deep @shared/* paths) and already drifts from the real surface. Remediation: delete it (lower effort) or wire it up + lint-forbid deep imports. Clears low bar.

counter_argument: Unreachable today; latent only if someone wires Tank.create up later.
rationale: Concrete dead code with a drift trap: Tank.create (0 callers) duplicates the TankState field list that createTank() owns, so a new TankState field silently staleifies it; Tank.bounds also dead. Remediation: delete both. Clears low bar.

counter_argument: Pure write-amplification; no correctness risk and the storage cost is small at hobby scale.
rationale: Concrete: room_actions has a redundant explicit index duplicating the UNIQUE(room_id,seq) constraint index — every INSERT maintains both. Remediation: DROP the explicit index in a new migration (the constraint index already serves the replay read path). Clears low bar.

counter_argument: At hobby scale the table grows slowly and the cost is negligible for a long time; only distinct-IP abuse accelerates it. Not a correctness issue.
rationale: Real unbounded growth: bump_rate_limit only cleans stale windows for the SAME bucket being incremented, so dead distinct-IP buckets persist forever (no TTL/cron). Remediation: a pg_cron/Edge-cron DELETE of rows older than 2 windows, or a created_at TTL.

counter_argument: No runtime defect — the live replay path uses the correct NetworkAction union from @shared/net/replay; this is a stale exported type + schema comment only.
rationale: Group supabase-typing: SupabaseTypes.RoomAction.action is typed NetworkFireAction only though the log carries 4 action types; 001_init.sql schema comment is likewise stale. Remediation: type it as the full NetworkAction union (+ update the SQL comment). (dx-004 is the duplicate of this.)

counter_argument: No runtime error today because ServiceClient is `any` (duck-typed). It is a misleading declared type, not a live bug.
rationale: Group supabase-typing: RoomRow omits status/seed/active_player_index/turn/winner/rematch_room_id though edge functions select('*') and read them. Remediation: extend RoomRow to all read columns (folds into the dx-001 typed-client fix).

POSITIVE CONTROL: submit_room_action correctly serializes via FOR UPDATE, allocates seq atomically, and commits/rolls back as one transaction with a TOCTOU guard + UNIQUE backstop. Recorded as evidence; nothing to fix.
POSITIVE CONTROL: migrations 001-005 are ordered, forward-only, non-destructive, with consistent RLS (public read / service-role write). Recorded as evidence; nothing to fix.

counter_argument: `any` is a pragmatic choice given supabase-js typing friction in Deno; the runtime works today. The risk is purely future schema drift going uncaught.
rationale: ANCHOR of group supabase-typing. ServiceClient=any makes every edge-function DB result untyped, so a column rename/missing field passes compilation and silently yields undefined/NaN (e.g. active_player_index -> NaN misroutes turn gating). Remediation: generate a Database type and use SupabaseClient<Database>; completing RoomRow (migration-005) and RoomAction (migration-003) fall out of this. effort M.
Identical to migration-003 (SupabaseTypes.RoomAction.action typed NetworkFireAction only). The migration lens recorded it first; deduped here. Resolved by the supabase-typing group.

counter_argument: Triggering needs an unknown weapon string, which an honest client never sends; a malicious member can only brick their OWN room (consistent with the no-auth posture). But version-skew (a new weapon replayed against an old client) is a non-malicious trigger, and the result is a hard crash, not mere log noise.
rationale: ANCHOR of group boundary-action-validation. NetworkFireAction/BuyAction.weapon is typed `string` and the referee does not validate it against the known weapon set; an unknown weapon string commits to the permanent log and crashes getWeapon() (undefined.detonation) on EVERY replaying client = permanent room brick. Security/reliability-relevant. Remediation: type weapon as WeaponType, validate against the known set in the referee, and guard getWeapon() to fail fast. effort S.

counter_argument: The casts are semantically backed by validateActionShape today (it does check typeof+isFinite); the risk is only that a future validator weakening would not surface at the cast site.
rationale: Group boundary-action-validation: the fire-branch `a.angle as number`/`as string` casts are sound-but-fragile assertions TS can't verify. Remediation: have validateActionShape return a narrowed (type-predicate) result so the construction is cast-free and a weakened validator is a compile error.

counter_argument: Enabling noUncheckedIndexedAccess will surface many call sites that are safe-by-construction, creating churn; the payoff is catching the rarer genuine footgun (e.g. the dx-003 weapon lookup).
rationale: Concrete strictness gap: tsconfig.base.json sets strict:true but not noUncheckedIndexedAccess, so Record/array indexing returns T not T|undefined — masking the exact undefined-on-unknown-key path behind dx-003. Remediation: enable the flag and address the surfaced sites. Pairs with boundary-action-validation. Clears low bar; effort M.

counter_argument: The cast is correct in practice (Number.isFinite(undefined)===false) and the change is a pure no-behavior cosmetic narrowing.
rationale: Real but trivial polish: replace `al as number` with `typeof al === 'number' && Number.isFinite(al)`. Below the issue bar; fold in opportunistically if GameEngine init is touched.

counter_argument: Requires a structurally-wrong 200 from the server, which our own edge functions don't produce; the guard is defense against contract drift, not a live bug.
rationale: Concrete: Lobby.ts uses non-null assertions (data.roomId!/code!/playerId!) on a json() cast with no presence check, so a malformed 200 yields empty room IDs that pass later guards and break Realtime silently. Remediation: explicit shape guard surfacing 'Unexpected server response'. Clears low bar; effort S.

counter_argument: A polished app would rarely throw to the top level; most failures are already handled inline. But on mobile an unhandled rejection is a blank screen with zero signal.
rationale: Concrete and high-value for diagnosis: main.ts has no window 'unhandledrejection'/'error' handler and startGame is fire-and-forget (void), so an init exception crashes silently. Remediation: add a global handler that surfaces a user message + console.error. effort S.

counter_argument: Overlaps reliability-005 (the hung-fetch variant); together they harden the same recoverStuckFire/resyncLog recovery path.
rationale: Group fire-recovery-robustness (with reliability-005): resyncLog failure is swallowed (logs + returns) so if _isFiring is still set the player stays stuck in 'Sending…'. Remediation: on resync failure/timeout release the fire lock + notify + set reconnecting. Pairs with reliability-005's AbortController timeout. effort S.

counter_argument: On its own it is just a log-context improvement; its value is realized together with reliability-006's recovery fix at the same code site.
rationale: Group tickcap-handling (with reliability-006): the tick-cap console.error carries no roomId/turn/seq, so the primary desync signal can't be correlated. Remediation: add {roomId,turn,seq} context AND make the cap path recover instead of wedging. effort S.

counter_argument: Near-identical to reliability-002; combined rather than marked duplicate because it adds the concrete bucket/IP context detail.
rationale: Group rate-limit-observability (with reliability-002): the fail-open path logs a bare error.message with no bucket/IP/function, indistinguishable from any DB error. Remediation: structured 'rate_limit: fail-open' log with {bucket, ip, error}. effort S.

counter_argument: 409 seq-conflicts are expected and self-healing; logging every one adds noise. Mitigated by keeping it at info level.
rationale: Group edge-logging-context: the 409 seq-conflict path emits zero server log, so a conflict flood is invisible. Remediation: info-level log with roomId + anonymized playerId. (Do alongside secrets-001 which trims the full-error logging on the same lines.)

counter_argument: At hobby scale per-room log correlation is rarely needed; bare errors are usually enough to notice an outage.
rationale: Group edge-logging-context: edge console.error calls omit the in-scope roomId/playerId, so a single broken room can't be isolated from the aggregated log. Remediation: thread {roomId} into the error logs. Pairs with observability-003 and secrets-001.

counter_argument: A 'Not your turn' rejection is sometimes benign (a legitimate race); logging it could create noise. But it is the canonical desync signature and currently dropped entirely.
rationale: Group desync-diagnostics: 'Not your turn' rejections are explicitly excluded from logging — the clearest local-vs-referee desync signal vanishes. Remediation: console.warn with {roomId, localActivePlayerId}, no user surface.

counter_argument: The 30s stall watchdog already gives a user-facing banner; this only adds a diagnostic log to distinguish gap-stall from idle-opponent.
rationale: Group desync-diagnostics: no diagnostic when pendingActions buffers behind a missing seq (Realtime delivery gap). Remediation: warn when pendingActions.size exceeds a small threshold or a gap persists, logging expected seq + buffered keys.

counter_argument: Provisional high overstates it: the engine harnesses cover the determinism core, and the untested functions are mostly thin CRUD. But ready_up's start transition, join_room's reap+capacity, and restart_game's claim/ai logic are genuinely branchy and unguarded.
rationale: ANCHOR of group edge-fn-test-coverage. 6 of 10 mutating edge functions (join_room, leave_room, ready_up, heartbeat, update_player, restart_game) have zero tests. Calibrated high->medium. Remediation: extract pure validators (the submit_action/validate.ts pattern) and add hermetic Deno tests; prioritize ready_up, restart_game, join_room. effort M.

counter_argument: finish_game fails open (returns ok regardless), so a sanitizer regression degrades the scoreboard record but does not break gameplay.
rationale: Group edge-fn-test-coverage: finish_game/sanitizeScoreboard (tankId regex, seat bounds, numeric coercion, name truncation) has zero tests. Remediation: export it and unit-test the valid/invalid/clamp/truncate paths. Pairs with appsec-002 (unverified winner). effort S.

counter_argument: The engine harness (turn_rotation.mjs) proves the engine skips dead seats; the referee gate's stale-cursor case is an edge condition that needs the referee-cursor-trust design decision to fully resolve.
rationale: Group edge-fn-test-coverage: authorizeAction has no test for a 3-4P game where active_player_index points to an eliminated seat. Remediation: add ghost-seat gate tests. Characterizes the referee-cursor-trust behavior (cross-ref that decision-required group). effort S.

counter_argument: nextCursor is pure-by-design (no liveness), so this 'pins behavior' rather than catching a bug; value is documentation + a tripwire if liveness is later added.
rationale: Group edge-fn-test-coverage: nextCursor has no test for the in-bounds-but-dead reported-seat case (honored, no liveness check). Remediation: pin the contract with a test + a comment that callers must validate liveness. Characterizes referee-cursor-trust.

counter_argument: Window rollover lives in SQL (hard to unit-test without a DB); the pure helpers (rateWindow/checkRateLimit) are already covered at their boundaries.
rationale: Group edge-fn-test-coverage: the window-rollover transition (capped in W, allowed in W+1) and stale-window cleanup are untested. Remediation: add boundary tests for rateWindow + a documented/integration check that bump_rate_limit(bucket,W) twice returns 1 then 2.

counter_argument: The array-vs-scalar branch is defensive; PostgREST currently returns one form consistently, so the untested branch is a guard, not a live path.
rationale: Group edge-fn-test-coverage: rpcResultToResponse handles both bare-number and [number] data but the test only covers the bare-number form. Remediation: add a data:[7] case. Small, fits the same edge-test effort.

counter_argument: The scripted determinism scenario fires a missile so fire is always [] — the gap is structural, not a current failure. A dedicated napalm determinism check would be needed to exercise it.
rationale: ANCHOR of group determinism-snapshot. determinism.mjs serialize() omits the `fire` FireCell[] field, so a napalm non-determinism regression would byte-match and pass silently. Remediation: add fire to the canonical snapshot + a napalm-shot determinism variant. The flagship determinism guard having blind spots is the high-value insight. effort S.

counter_argument: Dedicated harnesses (batteries.mjs, scoreboard.mjs, shield.mjs) DO cover these fields in isolation, so a regression isn't entirely uncovered — just not in the canonical double-run.
rationale: Group determinism-snapshot: the determinism.mjs tank snapshot omits all Sprint-4/5/6 fields (powerCap, shieldHp, credits, inventory, fuel, kills, totalDamage, roundWins, buried, buriedTurns); a divergence in any would pass the byte-comparison. Remediation: expand the tank snapshot to the engine-computed fields.

counter_argument: Single-round default scenario makes these fields trivially equal; rounds.mjs/netrounds.mjs cover multi-round. Low current risk.
rationale: Group determinism-snapshot: serialize() omits round/totalRounds/lastRoundWinnerId. Remediation: add them to the canonical snapshot (cheap, completes the determinism guard).

counter_argument: mapListedRoom doesn't read lastSeen so there's no functional gap today; the test validates mapListedRoom in isolation deliberately.
rationale: Real fixture drift with a coverage hole: list_rooms.test player() omits lastSeen, and the test calls mapListedRoom directly, bypassing the reap() that the real handler runs first — so a reap-pipeline regression is invisible. Remediation: add lastSeen to the fixture + a reap->mapListedRoom pipeline test with mixed stale/fresh players. Clears low bar.

counter_argument: authorizeAction reads only .id and .ai, never lastSeen, so the omission has zero functional effect today.
rationale: Real fixture drift but no current functional gap (validate.test human() omits lastSeen). Below the bar to file; fold into the determinism/edge-test work if those fixtures are touched.
